content: Processed foods are loaded with refined carbohydrates, which eliminate most nutrients during refinement, leading to an empty carbohydrate state that stimulates insulin production and causes a spike in blood sugar.

High fructose corn syrup in processed foods is more than just sugar; it is genetically modified and lacks nutritional value, potentially causing inflammation in the gut and affecting nutrient absorption.

Processed foods often contain MSG and other additives that create a hyper-reward sensation in the body, making consumers more likely to overeat and experience negative health effects such as fatigue and brain fog.
Artificial ingredients in processed foods are treated by the body as foreign objects, triggering an immune response that can lower resistance and make the body more susceptible to illness.
Processed foods have addictive properties due to their ability to trigger dopamine responses in the brain, leading to cravings and addiction, despite the negative impact on health.
